rationale: The subject property displays a complex hip roof with multiple facets. Northward ground shadows extend approximately 8–12 feet beyond the eave (estimated against the 50 ft scale bar), consistent with a moderate 6:12 pitch given the autumn sun angle in Thornton, CO (lat ~39.88°). The visible ridge is noticeably narrower than the building footprint, indicating meaningful rise but not an extreme steep pitch. Strong inter-plane shadow contrast and clear shingle texture visibility from near-nadir view further support a pitch above shallow (4:12) but below steep (8:12+). Northern Colorado's snow-climate context and the consistent hip geometry across all identical neighboring townhome units (all showing the same "4"-pattern shadow profile) corroborate a 6:12 estimate. A 7:12 cannot be fully ruled out, hence moderate confidence.
